refresh_rules.name: no_refresh
refresh_rules.proof: Decoder sees only the selected record stream; no extra state is needed.

refresh_rules.name: self_refresh_changed_windows
refresh_rules.proof: Any selected replacement creates new encoded bits at its stream position; later windows touching it are fresh by construction.

refresh_rules.name: equal_size_neutral_refresh
refresh_rules.proof: An equal-size seed record is a legal entry with the same output length, so selecting it changes current-layer bits without increasing output size.

refresh_rules.name: superposition_derived_refresh
refresh_rules.proof: Retained encoder-only variants are not serialized; when one is selected, the wire contains only the selected legal record.

refresh_rules.name: deterministic_bitstream_rechunk
refresh_rules.proof: After each layer, both encoder and decoder split the emitted bitstream into the next fixed entry schedule from the profile.

refresh_rules.name: deterministic_entry_permutation
refresh_rules.proof: A profile-selected layer permutation is inverted after decoding that layer; the 3-bit rule selector is charged per pass.

refresh_rules.name: layer_delimited_descriptor_refresh
refresh_rules.proof: A layer descriptor names the refresh profile and bit length; the descriptor cost is charged once per layer.

refresh_rules.name: phase_rotated_rechunk
refresh_rules.proof: The layer number selects a deterministic bit offset rotation; the decoder reverses it from the charged profile selector.

sweep_summary.recurrence_profile_selection: top bounded profiles plus best representatives for each required sweep axis

dominance_basis: pass 2 must reach target for any 10-effective-pass success
oracle_weighted_interval is the selection upper bound
the expanded one-byte lane dominates larger byte schedules for this ceiling in the tested profile space
byte-aligned literal initialization is charged explicitly and is no larger than the worst-case pad budget
fixed bitstream rechunking preserves charged bits while exposing smaller profile-known current entries
max retained variants 16 dominates smaller retained-state caps
equal-size and bloat-retained alternatives enabled dominate disabling either source of retained candidates
superposition_derived_refresh has the largest zero-metadata refresh coefficient in the modeled rule set

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Decoder splits each decoded layer bitstream into fixed 3-bit current entries from the profile; final residual length is implied by the layer bit length.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: Zero per-file bits for a fixed profile; multiplexed profile must charge a selector.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Decoder splits each decoded layer bitstream into fixed 4-bit current entries from the profile.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: Zero per-file bits for a fixed profile; multiplexed profile must charge a selector.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Decoder applies the fixed pass schedule: 3-bit rechunk after the first modeled layer, then 4-bit chunks for later layers.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: Zero per-file bits only when this schedule is the fixed decoder profile.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Decoder applies the fixed pass schedule: 4-bit rechunk, then 3-bit chunks for later layers.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: Zero per-file bits only when this schedule is the fixed decoder profile.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Pass 1 wraps larger raw blocks, then later profile rechunking exposes smaller current entries; raw bytes are recovered by normal recursive layer decode.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: Literal wrapper is charged in pass 1; rechunk profile is fixed or must be selected in metadata.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Encoder emits only the chosen non-overlapping seed/literal records; decoder follows the record stream and needs no selector side table.
Not a viability selector; decoder story is the ordinary emitted stream, but the selection estimate is an upper bound.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: No selector sidecar modeled, but result is labeled upper bound.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Encoder may keep up to four earned variants per position, then serializes only the selected collapsed path.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: Working state is compute/memory only; selected records are charged in output.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Decoder reverses the deterministic phase from the charged profile selector and layer number.
experiment_registry.charged_metadata: 3 bits per pass in the current proof-kernel rule.

experiment_registry.telomere_native_decode_story: Decoder uses the emitted record stream directly with no profile rechunk boundary change.
